frozen_components: Large language model (host model weights frozen during interpretation)
architecture.summary: The system combines mechanistic interpretability with Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) to identify and manipulate 'Repetition Features' in LLMs that drive repetitive text generation. Information flows: (1) logit analysis identifies layers involved in repetition; (2) SAE-based activation extraction isolates monosemantic repetition features; (3) activation manipulation (stimulation/deactivation) tests causal influence; (4) a repetition dataset and evaluation pipeline quantify feature effects on repetition behavior.
trained_components: Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) for monosemantic feature extraction

biblio.title: Understanding the Repeat Curse in Large Language Models from a Feature Perspective

claims.statement: The Repeat Curse in LLMs can be investigated through the lens of mechanistic interpretability by identifying key model activations (Repetition Features) responsible for generating repetitive outputs
Logit analysis can locate the layers most involved in repetition
SAE-based activation manipulation enables extraction and stimulation of Repetition Features
A repetition dataset covering token and paragraph level repetitions and an evaluation pipeline can quantify the influence of identified repetition features
Deactivating Repetition Features effectively mitigates the Repeat Curse
